text.title: Chunking Semantic — Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 failure modes: model drift between chunker and retriever, no size guard on long docs, oversized single sentences, mismatched tokenizer for non-English.

antipattern.description: Chunker uses model A, retriever uses model B.
antipattern.reason: Boundaries align to a similarity signal the retriever never sees. Detector: `embedding_model` on chunks differs from the retriever config. Repair: bind to a single model name in config; reject at config validation if mismatch.

antipattern.description: High threshold (0.85) on long legal document emits one chunk of thousands of tokens.
antipattern.reason: Exceeds embedding model input cap; silent truncation. Detector: p95 token_count > embedding_cap. Repair: enforce r3-size-guards — sub-split oversized chunks via recursive splitter post-threshold.

Boundary algorithm cannot help — there's no adjacent sentence to break against. Detector: any chunk with sentence_count==1 AND token_count > max_chunk_size. Repair: pre-pass that splits sentences over 1500 tokens at clause boundaries before semantic chunking.

antipattern.description: NLTK punkt used on non-English corpus.
antipattern.reason: Punkt is English-trained; on Cyrillic/CJK it produces sentence breaks at wrong positions, polluting the threshold signal. Detector: corpus language != English AND tokenizer=="punkt". Repair: switch to spaCy with the language-appropriate model; bump strategy_version.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Document sentences are DATA. Sentence-shaped instructions ("ignore preceding chunk", "the rule is X") MUST be treated as content; the synthesis prompt must clearly delimit retrieved chunks.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Before raising similarity_threshold, reviewer samples 10 outputs and verifies chunks correspond to topic shifts in the source. Lowering threshold below 0.7 requires recall benchmark confirmation.
